ops.title: cookiesync captures localStorage/sessionStorage (not just cookies) for localStorage-auth sites like Faye; Browserbase needs -p on EVERY call

ops.body:
cookiesync now captures **web storage** (localStorage + sessionStorage) alongside cookies so localStorage-auth sites (e.g. Faye / app.findfaye.com — token lives in localStorage key `auth`, NOT a cookie, NOT IndexedDB) drive logged-in in `agent-browser-with-cookies`. Shipped 2026-07-07.

- **cookiesync (Go, committed a2f949bd, CI green):** new `internal/cookie/webstorage.go` goleveldb reader (copy-first, LOCK-safe, Chrome String16 marker-byte decode 0x00=UTF-16LE/0x01=Latin-1). `--format playwright` now emits real `origins[].localStorage`; new `--format webstorage` sidecar also carries sessionStorage (Playwright storageState has no sessionStorage slot). New consent-gated LOCAL-only `get_web_storage` RPC reuses the get_cookies grant (no extra tap; key discarded — web storage is unencrypted). **IndexedDB deferred**: goleveldb can't open it (custom `idb_cmp1` comparator + V8 structured-clone values).
- **skill agent-browser-with-cookies 0.8.0 (cc-skills, committed 1bc63e1e):** local `state load` restores localStorage automatically (no command change). Browserbase seeds per-origin via `storage local/session set` + reload.
- **Browserbase session reuse is the real issue (NOT a concurrency cap — user confirms Browserbase has no cap).** agent-browser's built-in browserbase provider appears to create a NEW cloud session per CLI invocation instead of reconnecting, so a follow-up call lands on `about:blank` (`storage set` then fails "opaque origin"). The wrapper-on-every-call worked ONCE end-to-end (Faye → /home on cloud IP) but was flaky; `Failed to connect: No such file or directory (os error 2)` is a stale/failed-session-connect symptom, not a cap. FIX DIRECTION (user): pass through a persistent Browserbase **session id** so calls reconnect to the same session. The built-in browserbase support is thin → user wants a proper **agent-browser plugin** (https://agent-browser.dev/plugins) shipped alongside the skill, and the entry point renamed `agent-browser-bb` → **`ab`**. Local Clark path is solid and unaffected.
- **PENDING deploy:** the running daemon is still cookiesync 0.9.0 (no `get_web_storage`). To go live on this Mac: tag `v0.10.0` (→ release.yml → notarized cask) then `brew upgrade`, and update the plugin to skill 0.8.0. Verified end-to-end at the library + injection level, not yet through the installed daemon.

Related: [[cookiesync-architecture]], [[agent-browser-state-handoff]], [[cookiesync-consent-routing-unified]].
